mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations of RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher rates, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods consistently precede negative 5d returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend signals expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60d forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above threshold consistently coincide with positive 20d market returns over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in housing starts during late-cycle expansions signal collapsing consumer confidence and credit stress, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Housing start declines fail to precede negative 5d returns in late-cycle periods across historical samples.
